content: Curiosity-driven active learning agents can use a HyDE-inspired hypothetical memory generation mechanism to bootstrap retrieval of more relevant past experiences when exploring open-ended environments. Instead of generating a hypothetical document to align query embedding for external knowledge retrieval, the agent generates a hypothetical summary of what a useful past memory of a similar situation would look like, then uses this hypothetical embedding to retrieve actual stored memories that match the implicit contextual structure of the problem.
